collect-sources.prompt:
# Upgrade Feed Collection — Add Alignment Forum + arXiv + LessWrong

Read existing feed system:
- skills/feed-recommend/scripts/feed.py
- skills/feed-recommend/scripts/feed_engine.py
- skills/feed-recommend/SKILL.md (if exists)
- memory/feeds/config.json
- memory/hn/candidates/ (example format)

Create skills/feed-recommend/scripts/source_alignment_forum.py:
- Fetch recent posts from Alignment Forum (https://www.alignmentforum.org)
- Use their GraphQL API or RSS feed (https://www.alignmentforum.org/feed.xml)
- Extract: title, author, url, date, score/karma, summary (first 200 chars)
- Output JSONL to stdout (same schema as HN candidates)
- Support --since YYYY-MM-DD flag
- Support --limit N flag (default 20)

Create skills/feed-recommend/scripts/source_lesswrong.py:
- Same as above but for LessWrong (https://www.lesswrong.com/feed.xml)
- Filter for AI-related posts (tag-based if available)

Create skills/feed-recommend/scripts/source_arxiv.py:
- Search arXiv for recent papers in: cs.AI, cs.CL, cs.LG, cs.SD
- Use arXiv API (http://export.arxiv.org/api/query)
- Keywords: 'interpretability', 'mechanistic', 'safety', 'alignment', 'speech', 'audio', 'SAE', 'sparse autoencoder'
- Output same JSONL schema
- Support --days N (default 3), --max-results N (default 30)

Create tests for all three: skills/feed-recommend/scripts/test_sources.py
- Mock HTTP responses, test parsing, test JSONL output format
- At least 3 tests per source

Run tests to verify.

smart-scorer.prompt:
# Build Smart Relevance Scorer

Read existing scoring:
- skills/feed-recommend/scripts/feed_engine.py (current scoring logic)
- skills/feed-recommend/scripts/quality_scorer.py
- memory/feeds/preferences.json (Leo's interest profile)

Create skills/feed-recommend/scripts/smart_scorer.py:
- Takes JSONL candidates from stdin or --input file
- Scores each item 1-10 on relevance to Leo's research interests:
  * Primary (weight 3x): mechanistic interpretability, SAE, speech/audio ML, AI safety, alignment
  * Secondary (weight 2x): LLM internals, transformer architecture, probing, evaluation methods
  * Tertiary (weight 1x): Python tooling, research methodology, open source AI, ML engineering
- Scoring method: keyword matching + title/summary analysis (no LLM API call — fast & free)
- Deduplicates against memory/feeds/seen.jsonl (by URL)
- Outputs scored + sorted JSONL to stdout
- Support --top N (default 10) to output only top N
- Support --min-score N (default 3) to filter low-relevance

Create skills/feed-recommend/scripts/test_smart_scorer.py (5+ tests)
Run tests.

digest-formatter.prompt:
# Build Digest Formatter + Delivery

Read existing:
- skills/feed-recommend/scripts/feed.py (current digest format)
- memory/hn/candidates/2026-03-18.jsonl (example data)

Create skills/feed-recommend/scripts/digest_formatter.py:
- Takes scored JSONL from stdin or --input file
- Groups items by category:
  * 🔬 AI Safety & Alignment
  * 🧠 Interpretability & Mechanistic
  * 🔊 Speech & Audio ML
  * 🛠️ Engineering & Tools
  * 🌶️ General Interest
- Formats as Discord-friendly markdown (no tables, use bullets)
- Each item: title (linked), score badge, 1-line summary, source tag [HN|AF|LW|arXiv]
- Header: date + total candidates scanned + top N selected
- Support --format discord (default) | --format email | --format json

Create skills/feed-recommend/scripts/daily_feed_pipeline.sh:
- End-to-end pipeline script that:
  1. Collects from all sources (HN candidates + AF + LW + arXiv) in parallel
  2. Merges all JSONL into one stream
  3. Pipes through smart_scorer.py --top 10
  4. Pipes through digest_formatter.py --format discord
  5. Saves digest to memory/feeds/digests/YYYY-MM-DD.json
  6. Marks seen URLs in memory/feeds/seen.jsonl
  7. Outputs formatted digest to stdout
- Make it executable (chmod +x)
- Support --dry-run (collect + score but don't mark seen)
- Support --send-discord (sends to Leo DM via openclaw system event)

Create tests: skills/feed-recommend/scripts/test_digest_formatter.py (5+ tests)
Run tests.

update-cron.prompt:
# Update Cron for New Feed Pipeline

Read:
- skills/feed-recommend/scripts/daily_feed_pipeline.sh (just created by previous CC)
- Current cron setup: check 'openclaw cron list' output

Update the existing HN daily digest cron job to use the new pipeline:
1. Run: openclaw cron list — find the HN digest job
2. If it exists on this machine (Mac), update it to run daily_feed_pipeline.sh
3. If it only exists on Lab, create a note in memory/2026-03-19.md about updating Lab cron

Also update skills/feed-recommend/SKILL.md:
- Document the new multi-source pipeline
- List all scripts and their roles
- Add quick-start: how to run manually
- Add cron setup instructions

Do NOT actually send any Discord messages or run the pipeline — just set up the cron and docs.
